params.name: Benin Plaque
params.description:
On Sept 18, 1500 AD, the Benin Plaque was recorded by an unnamed court minister.
It is made of brass in present-day Nigeria. Each plaque is as large as a painting and depicts the war victory scenes and court ceremonies of Benin ruler Oba using deep relief carving. They are top-notch works of art, extraordinary metal sculptures, and witnesses to the history of European-African contact. From the peaceful trade of the first contact to the bloody massacre of the second contact, they still remind …
